# Code of Conduct

This project adopts the **Contributor Covenant, version 2.1**, as its code of conduct.

The full text is published at:
**https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/**

A plain-language summary: contributors and maintainers commit to making participation a welcoming, harassment-free experience for everyone. Disrespectful or abusive behavior toward other contributors is not tolerated, and maintainers are expected to enforce these standards fairly.

## Reporting

If you experience or witness behavior that violates this code, contact the maintainer:

- **Email:** dpittma8@msudenver.edu
- **GitHub:** [@daniel-pittman](https://github.com/daniel-pittman)

Reports will be reviewed promptly and handled with discretion. The reporter's identity will not be disclosed without their consent.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all project spaces — issues, pull requests, discussions, the project's code itself — and also when an individual is officially representing the project in public spaces.

## Enforcement

Maintainers are responsible for clarifying and enforcing the standards in the linked Contributor Covenant document. They may remove, edit, or reject contributions that don't align with it, and may temporarily or permanently ban contributors for behaviors deemed inappropriate.

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct adopts the **Contributor Covenant** (https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1, by reference.
